note: SMOKE tier (v1 542 clips, 2ep on 5090): 1.13B+MegatronLoRA fair free-decode. Best cer_norm 0.4614 @ep0; ep1 overfit to 0.4983 (tiny data). Beats all prior fair OURS numbers; XL round next.

SCREEN tier (partial-XL 3207 train clips, 2ep, grad-clip 1.0, lr 1e-4): fair cer_norm 0.0759 — TOPS the open-ALM leaderboard (Voxtral 0.187). ep0 0.1612 → ep1 0.0759, no overfit. Confirm tier on full XL next.

200M XL (10k clips, 3ep, EOS-weighted, grad-clip): best fair cer_norm 0.624 @ep1 (ep0 0.770, ep2 overfit 0.945 — best-ckpt kept ep1). Improves instruct+EOS 1.03 but plateaus far above the 1B 0.05 — the leaderboard win is scale+data+EOS, not the 200M core.
CONFIRM tier: 1.13B+LoRA on full 10k XL corpus, best fair cer_norm 0.0392 @ep1 — decisive #1 vs open ALMs (Voxtral 0.187), 4.8x lower. Data curve 0.46->0.076->0.039. Single seed.

PUBLIC TESTS (full, per-clip): sa-Shrutilipi ours WER 1.10[1.04-1.17] BEATS Whisper-lg-v3 1.33[1.26-1.40] (non-overlapping CI); chant CER 0.31; en-LibriSpeech ours WER 1.10 = NOT competitive (English-shaped babble — weak audio conditioning, capacity hypothesis). v4 lever running: r=64 (4x adapter), EN-2x-weighted, 4ep. Remaining baselines: qwen/voxtral/sushrota sa + whisper/qwen/voxtral en.

v4 iteration chain: (a) cold r=64 LoRA + lr1e-4 diverged — single non-finite step at ~14k poisoned Adam (loss 1.98→1.87 then NaN); NaN guard added. (b) guarded cold rerun: finite loss 2.43 but EOS-collapse (val CER exactly 1.0 = empty preds) — cold high-rank adapter loses all competence. (c) FIX: rank-expansion warm-start r16→r64, function-preserving (unit diff 0.0), extra 48 ranks free + EN-2x weighting; relaunched, 144 tensors warm.

v5 (r=64 rank-expansion warm, EN-2x, lr 2.5e-5, 4ep) FINAL: en_val 1.53/0.80/0.93/0.86, sa_val 0.57/0.535/0.561/0.556, macro 1.054/0.668/0.748/0.709. Best en 0.8016 == v3 0.799 (no material gain). Save bar held: bi1b_ckpt.pt md5-identical to v3 backup, never clobbered. H-capacity FALSIFIED across three levers (epochs v3, rank v4-cold, rank+weight+stable-lr v5). Now probing H-prior: frozen-core teacher-forced byte CE on English vs Sanskrit gold text (no audio).

DECISIVE + SURPRISING: teacher-forced byte CE on gold text, frozen core alone: English 1.873 nats/byte vs Sanskrit 2.675 — the core models ENGLISH BETTER than Sanskrit. H-prior FALSIFIED (my own hypothesis). With the trained bilingual LoRA loaded: English 3.841 (WORSE than no adapter, 1.873) while Sanskrit improves 2.675->1.507. So the shared adapter ACTIVELY DEGRADES English below the untouched core = catastrophic language interference, not capacity (v4/v5) and not prior. Prediction under test: attenuating LoRA should IMPROVE English and hurt Sanskrit (crossing curves) => per-language adapter routing is the lever.

MY ROUTING PREDICTION FALSIFIED. Scale 1.0: en 0.8035 / sa 0.5181 (reproduces v3). Scale 0.75: en 5.3743 / sa 0.7978 — attenuation destroys BOTH languages (en CER>5 = runaway decode, output ~6x gold length). The projector was trained jointly WITH the full-scale adapter, so the adapter is load-bearing for the audio->core interface; you cannot recover English by turning it down. CONCLUSION: interference is real in TEXT space (core_prior_probe) but inference-time per-language scale routing is NOT a viable fix. A true fix would need per-language adapters trained jointly with the projector — which abandons the one-model-two-languages (sphota-unity) hypothesis rather than supporting it. Honest closure: unified bilingual claim NOT supported; Sanskrit public claim strongly supported.

FAIRNESS DEFECT FOUND IN OUR OWN HARNESS: every baseline gets explicit language conditioning (whisper language=sanskrit/english, voxtral hi/en, qwen "Transcribe this Sanskrit/English speech") but ours got the SAME untagged "transcribe the speech" for both languages. We handicapped ourselves AND withheld the one signal a shared adapter needs to separate the two language regimes — which is exactly the interference mechanism measured in core_prior_probe. v6 lever: language-tagged instructions (transcribe the english/sanskrit speech) at train+eval, r=16 lr 1e-4 (proven-stable regime; r=64 falsified), warm from v3, EN-2x, 4ep. Ckpts carry lang_tagged flag so eval never mismatches the trained prompt. Mechanism-derived and unity-preserving (still ONE model, ONE adapter). Also stopped the scale sweep after 1.0/0.75 — attenuation destroys the audio path, conclusion established.
